title: POST /api/v1/apps: register client app, return credentials
description:
Implement Mastodon application registration. Accept: client_name, redirect_uris, scopes, website. Generate client_id (UUID v4) and client_secret (32 random bytes base64url). Store in oauth_apps table. Return Application object matching Mastodon's shape.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to POST to mastodon.social /api/v1/apps and document the exact response structure as the oracle.

**Test discipline**:
- Register app: verify client_id is UUID format, client_secret is base64url
- Missing client_name: 422 with clear error
- Empty redirect_uris: 422
- Verify response shape against mastodon.social oracle (all fields present, correct types)
mastodon.social response captured externally is the oracle — never the implementation testing itself.

**Defensive programming**:
- client_secret generated with rand::rngs::OsRng, not thread_rng
- vapid_key: return empty string (not null, not absent) — clients check for this field
- Validate redirect_uris is a non-empty string before accepting
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors Result<T, AppError>
acceptance_criteria: client_id is UUID format; client_secret is cryptographically random base64url from OsRng; vapid_key present as empty string not null; response shape matches mastodon.social oracle; missing fields return 422
